result.diagram_code:
sequenceDiagram
    actor Att as Attacker
    participant EK as process_encK
    participant S as Server
    participant Init as Initiator(xA = B)
    participant Resp as Responder(xB = B)

    alt Leak of secretA
        Att->>S: request certs for (B, B)
        S-->>Att: certSignB = sign((B, spkB), skS)\ncertEncB = sign((B, pkB), skS)
        Att->>EK: register enc_keys(B, pk(att_sk))
        Att->>S: request certs for (dummy, B)
        S-->>Att: dummy cert\ncertEncB* = sign((B, pk(att_sk)), skS)
        Att->>Init: start session with xA = B
        Att->>Init: certSignB, certEncB*
        Init-->>Att: encrypt(sign((B, B, k), sskB), pk(att_sk))
        Init-->>Att: sencrypt(secretA, k)
        Att->>Att: decrypt first message, recover k, then decrypt secretA
    else Leak of secretB
        Att->>EK: register enc_keys(A, spk(att_ssk))
        Att->>S: request certs for (dummy, A)
        S-->>Att: dummy cert\ncertA* = sign((A, spk(att_ssk)), skS)
        Att->>S: request certs for (dummy, B)
        S-->>Att: dummy cert\ncertEncB = sign((B, pkB), skS)
        Att->>Resp: start session with xB = B
        Att->>Resp: certA* as cert1, certEncB as cert2,\nencrypt(sign((A, B, k), att_ssk), pkB)
        Resp-->>Att: sencrypt(secretB, k)
        Att->>Att: use known k to decrypt secretB
    end
result.explanation: The main bug is that process_encK accepts attacker-chosen encryption keys for any host, including honest A and B. The server then signs these attacker-controlled bindings as valid certificates. In the trace for secretA, the initiator encrypts the fresh session key to an attacker key certified for B, so the attacker recovers k and decrypts secretA. In the trace for secretB, the attacker obtains a server-signed certificate for A containing an attacker verification key and reuses it to make the responder accept a forged signed session key, leaking secretB.
result.notes: The secretB trace also shows certificate-type confusion: certificates are just signed pairs, so an encryption-key certificate can be replayed where a signing-key certificate is expected. Adding explicit tags for sign-key vs enc-key certificates would harden the model further.
